text: Abstract Agrarian structure in Poland has undergone substantial transformations over recent decades, yet its current spatial structure remains a phenomenon that has not been fully explored. In this paper we endeavour to describe the spatial structure of contemporary Polish agriculture on the basis of the Agricultural Census of 2010. By examining the influence of selected groups of internal agricultural factors on the degree of development of Polish agriculture (including land quality and use, agrarian structure, socio-demographic factors, technical equipment, the structure of agricultural production and the situation in the agricultural household), we derived a composite index that can be considered a synthetic indicator of the state of agriculture. An analysis of the relations between the spatial structure of Polish agriculture and the investigated external factors such as natural factors, historical factors, urbanization and direct support under the CAP was also conducted. It was established that the spatial structure of Polish agriculture is formed under highly diverse conditions and that the natural factors only have an insignificant impact on it. Of the human-caused factors, the most important of the investigated factors that determine the current spatial structure of agriculture are historical structures of particular regions, which –despite the significant absorption of EU support – are still visible and unchanged.
